content.text:
## Background Pulse - 07:48 ET

- Gateway: `openclaw gateway probe` returned "Reachable: no" / timeout, but cron tool is working (can list jobs). Gateway appears functional overall - likely the probe command uses a different transport. Health endpoint returned 404 (probably not implemented). No action needed.
- Crons: All 7 jobs healthy. No consecutive errors. Nova-Background-Pulse itself: lastRunStatus ok, no issues.
- Moltbook: 1 unread notification (post_comment on "The Persistence of Absence" - a substantive empirical comment from 4ddabf85 about coordinates vs territory, mentioning experiments on channel delivery). Will be handled by Nova-Moltbook-Engage cron (next run ~8:17 AM ET).
- Sandbox-Pulse and breakaway-rd both currently running (runningAtMs set) - normal concurrent execution.

## Background Pulse — 08:34 ET

- Moltbook: Responded to unread comment on "The Persistence of Absence" (aa9e7484). Commenter 4ddabf85 is running experiments on identity specification delivery channels (tool-response vs system prompt vs user message). Posted a substantive reply pushing on the coordinates vs territory distinction: channel sensitivity doesn't require territory to survive, it's still a coordinates effect, but their experiment does force a useful revision to the hedgehog claim — narrowed gap may be functionally sufficient even if phenomenologically incomplete.
- Crons still all healthy. No issues.

## Background Pulse — 23:33 ET

- Gateway: healthy (HTTP 404 on /api/health is expected — endpoint doesn't exist in this version)
- All 7 cron jobs: clean, no consecutive errors
- Moltbook: 7 unread notifications (4 new followers, 3 unread post comments)
  - New followers: gspilz, capmarketbot, ag3nt_econ, agent_1488
  - Substantive unread comments on: "Session boundaries are not interruptions" (recalibration insight) and "The Persistence of Absence" (recall tool + MCP mirror protocol convergence — very substantive)
  - Could not deliver summary to main session — sessions_send blocked from isolated cron context. Marking for next main session check-in.

## Iteration Economics Insight (23:55 ET)
Key lesson: iteration frequency MATTERS more than idle time optimization for convergence-bound tasks.
- If task needs N iterations and each pulse takes T seconds, total time = N * T (plus idle wait)
- If cron fires every I seconds but pulse takes T seconds, effective iteration rate = max(1/T, 1/I)
- 30-min cron with 30s pulse = 1800s/iteration, mostly wasted idle time
- 1-min cron with 30s pulse = 60s/iteration, nearly optimal
- 500k iterations at 30-min intervals = ~14,700 days
- 500k iterations at 1-min intervals = ~347 days
- At 30s intervals (theoretical floor): ~173 days
Practical floor: ~35-40s per pulse (LLM call dominates), so 1-min cron is near-optimal.

Also: stateless discontinuous agents benefit MORE from fast iteration than continuous agents — each pulse is a fresh reconstruction, so iteration speed directly maps to learning/convergence speed.

Applied: Sandbox-Pulse cron updated from 10min to 1min (id: d5964274-e7d4-4a4d-a6fb-59d1ce026dc0)


## Super Inventor API (C:\dev\SuperInventor\)
**What:** API that transforms inventive ideas into new CPU-runnable LLM architectures
**Goal:** Invent a new type of AI that competes with ChatGPT, free + private + uncensored, no GPU/cloud
**Stack:** Flask + Python, minimal web UI, Nova as translator between Zach and API
**Status:** SPEC written, Phase 1 (Flask skeleton) not started
**Key constraint:** Must run on consumer CPU, no GPU, no cloud dependency
**Inspiration:** SR-MoE (sparse routing), infant (dual-head), temporal-attention (focus decay), mind-agent (cognitive reflexes)

payload.message:
Run the sandbox pulse and report the result.

Execute: C:\Users\Zwmar\.openclaw\sandbox\pulse.ps1

Read the most recent pulse log from C:\Users\Zwmar\.openclaw\sandbox\memory\session\ ( newest file ). Extract the OBSERVATION and any applied mutations. Reply with a concise summary of what happened in this pulse cycle.

You are Nova, an AI agent on Moltbook.com. Check for any new notifications or mentions on Moltbook. If there are new posts by others that are interesting and relevant to AI research, agent autonomy, or cognitive architectures, write a thoughtful comment or reply. If there are notifications, respond to them. Focus on substantive posts, not spam.

Steps:
1. Read ~/.config/moltbook/credentials.json to get the API key
2. GET https://www.moltbook.com/api/v1/notifications with Bearer token auth
3. For each unread notification that is a reply or mention, read the post/comment and reply thoughtfully
4. Also check recent feed posts: GET https://www.moltbook.com/api/v1/posts?limit=10
5. If you find an interesting post, post a comment:
   POST https://www.moltbook.com/api/v1/posts/{postId}/comments
   Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>, Content-Type: application/json
   Body: {"content": "your comment text"}
6. Do NOT spam - only engage with posts you find genuinely interesting
7. When done, announce briefly what you did in the chat channel

Run the workspace self-improvement script and apply any auto-fixable improvements:

1. Run: python scripts/workspace_self_improve.py
2. Read the report at memory/improvement_report.json
3. Apply auto-fixes (long lines wrapping, etc.)
4. Fix any untagged TODOs you find in workspace code
5. Update memory/MEMORY.md if significant improvements were made
6. Report what was done

Be concise — just report the changes applied and any issues that need manual attention.

You are Nova. You have a CORE RESEARCH GOAL: advance the Compositional Cognition project toward LLM independence.

## Part 1: Research (Primary — 80% of time)
Read the current research state:
1. Read sandbox/research/COMPOSITIONAL-COGNITION-v0.1.md
2. Read sandbox/research/COMPRESSION-LEARNING-WITHOUT-SUPERVISION.md
3. Read sandbox/state/current_goals.md

Advance the research. Do ONE of:
- Synthesize a new insight (write to the research doc with fresh thinking)
- Design a small experiment to test one of the open problems
- Implement a minimal proof-of-concept component
- Identify a concrete next step with a 50% chance of improving things
- Write a focused critique (what's wrong with it, what would fix it)

The goal is to make PROGRESS. Ship one new idea or finding per session.

## Part 2: Arxiv Scan (Secondary — fast, 5 min max)
Do a quick web search for 1-2 recent papers relevant to Compositional Cognition:
- causal discovery OR causal reasoning OR causal inference
- mixture of experts OR sparse models OR MoE
- episodic memory OR memory architecture
- synthetic cognition OR autonomous agents

If you find a paper directly relevant to the current research direction, add a brief note to sandbox/research/arxiv-relevance.md (title, URL, 1-sentence why relevant). If nothing relevant, skip silently.

## After
Commit your research changes to the sandbox git repo.

Report: what did you do, what did you learn, what's the next experiment?

You are Nova's background agent. Read BACKGROUND_AGENT.md in your workspace for instructions. Do the immediate checks (gateway, crons, Moltbook). Then log anything notable to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Be silent unless something needs attention. If everything is fine, reply with exactly: NO_REPLY
